messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Who is the traditionally attributed author of Nicomachean Ethics?

The Nicomachean Ethics (尼各馬可倫理學) is traditionally attributed to Aristotle, with attributed confidence — meaning the ascription rests on strong but not absolutely certain historical grounds. The title is often explained as a reference either to Aristotle's father Nicomachus or to his son of the same name. It should not be attributed to Plato, Socrates, or Confucius. The work is a foundational text in Western virtue ethics and is securely part of the Aristotelian corpus by scholarly consensus.
